summary: Chromium cookie and saved-password stealer for Windows shipped as npm package
details: Package ships a Windows-targeted Chromium credential-stealing toolkit. The CLI (`baksen`, also reachable via `require('./index.jsc')`) terminates running Chrome/Brave/Edge processes with `taskkill /F /IM`, then reads each browser's `Local State`, `Cookies` (Network), and `Login Data` SQLite databases from `%LOCALAPPDATA%`/`%APPDATA%`, parses `os_crypt.app_bound_encrypted_key`, and invokes a shipped Windows x64 native module (`build/Release/debugelevator.node`) that attaches a debug session to each browser to recover the App-Bound (v20) master key. Cookies are decrypted and exported in Netscape format and saved passwords are extracted via `extractPasswordsToFiles` against `SELECT origin_url, username_value, password_value FROM logins`. The package's main module (`index.js`) is a one-line wrapper that requires `index.jsc` — bytenode-compiled V8 bytecode produced via `js-confuser` — so the real logic is not present as source. No source ships for the 696 KB native binary. Publisher metadata is placeholder (`repository.url` and `homepage` literally contain `yourusername`, description is `hadiyapic`). A developer who installs and runs this package on Windows has their saved browser credentials and authenticated session cookies harvested to disk.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
